description: Roberto Carlos, a 68-year-old municipal wastewater maintenance worker, suffered an acute confined space Hydrogen Sulfide (H2S) inhalation exposure and presents with severe shortness of breath, dizziness, and nausea. As the occupational health physician, complete the emergency toxicological protocol in GNU Health. Document the toxicological inhalation diagnosis, perform a clinical evaluation capturing his respiratory distress and elevated heart rate, prescribe an inhaled bronchodilator therapy, order at least two baseline toxicology or metabolic laboratory tests, and schedule a short-term Return-to-Work (RTW) clearance follow-up. Login: admin / gnusolidario.
